pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh (lines 10, 15), export_result.sh (lines 13-14), verifier.py (implied by output paths), and task.json metadata.
Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 11), export_result.sh (line 13), verifier.py (line 182), and task.json metadata.
Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 12), export_result.sh (line 14), verifier.py (line 77), and task.json metadata.

Verified through medical literature. Pneumocephalus (intracranial air) has HU value of approximately -1000 (source: PMC2908654, radiopaedia.org). A maximum threshold of -200 HU captures air while excluding soft tissue (brain parenchyma: 20-45 HU per medical sources). Confirmed in setup_task.sh radiology request (line 35) and README.md (line 18).

Confirmed in README.md (line 20) and setup_task.sh radiology request (line 38). Medical literature shows brain parenchyma (white matter 20-30 HU, gray matter 37-45 HU per fpnotebook.com) and CSF (10-15 HU per multiple sources). A minimum threshold of -200 HU includes brain tissue while potentially excluding lower-density structures.

Confirmed in task.json description (line 4) and README.md (line 20). Medical literature shows gray matter peaks at ~45 HU. A maximum threshold of >= 50 HU includes normal brain parenchyma and provides safety margin. Confirmed by InVesalius soft tissue preset documentation (soft tissue preset is -700–225 HU in InVesalius User Manual).
Confirmed in task.json description (line 5), README.md (line 21, 42, 66), and verifier.py (lines 40, 189-190). All sources consistently require >= 4 measurements.

Confirmed in task.json metadata and README.md (line 93). Medical literature shows frontal sinuses hold 4-7 mL and ethmoid air cells are 2-3 mL with 5-15 air cells per side (sources: clevelandclinic.org, ncbi.nlm.nih.gov). A 5 mm minimum measurement is reasonable for individual air cells and sinus cavity dimensions. README explicitly states 'Measurement range: 5–80 mm for sinus/air cavity dimensions'.

Verified from multiple sources: (1) setup_invesalius.sh lines 71-73 downloads https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip; (2) setup_task.sh line 9 references '/home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium'; (3) export_skull_stl validated_pi.json confirms dataset case ID '0051' from official InVesalius v3.0 release. This is the CT Cranium sample dataset from InVesalius official repository.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires a radiologist to segment intracranial air (pneumocephalus) and brain soft tissue from a CT Cranium DICOM scan using InVesalius 3 software. The dataset is the official CT Cranium sample (case 0051) from InVesalius v3.0 release (https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip), which is real medical imaging data, not synthetic. The air segmentation uses Hounsfield Unit (HU) threshold with maximum -200 (air is approximately -1000 HU while brain tissue is 20-45 HU), the soft tissue mask uses HU range minimum -200 and maximum >= 50 to capture brain parenchyma, measurements must be >= 4 with each measurement >= 5 mm to document sinus and air pocket dimensions (typical sinus dimensions are 4-7 mL, making 5 mm measurements appropriate for individual air cells), outputs are STL format for 3D surface mesh and .inv3 InVesalius project file saved to specified paths in /home/ga/Documents/air_analysis/ directory. All threshold values are medically sound and consistent with CT density standards for air, CSF, and brain tissue.
